verdict_reason: The single disagreement (pūjārhau) is well-explained. It identifies the contested term, names the agreement on literal sense (worthy of worship), but specifies the commentarial weight differs — Shankara emphasizes the structural-positional reading (ritual veneration; Bhīṣma/Droṇa occupy positions that demand respect), while a more affective reading is also possible. The doctrinal axis (whether the worship is structural or affective) is named, which is doctrinally substantive — different readings produce different diagnoses of how Arjuna's loyalty is misdirected (towards a position vs. towards a person). One tight line, doctrinally substantive, no winner picked. Strong quality. Slightly below gold-standard because the alternative position is not as crisply named as the Shankara position.
